history.content:
<thought>
The goal G is visible two cells to the right and three cells ahead. The cell directly in front of me is a floor cell, and the path to the goal involves moving forward and then navigating around the lava cells on the right. It is safe to move forward first.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal G is visible two cells ahead and two cells to the right. Directly in front of me are lava cells, so moving forward is not safe. I will try to turn right to explore that path as it may lead me safely towards the goal or a better route.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The goal G is visible one cell ahead and one cell to the left. However, there is lava L directly in front of me blocking forward movement. Turning left will face me towards the goal cell G which is safe. 
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I see the goal G is two cells ahead and two cells to the right. However, the cells directly in front and to the front-right are lava (L), which are deadly and must be avoided. Since I cannot move forward safely, I should turn right to face a safer direction and explore potential paths around the lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
In front of me is a wall (#), so I cannot move forward. The goal G is located two cells ahead and one cell to the left of my current position, but there is lava (L) to the left as well. To get closer safely, I should turn left first to explore that direction for a path around the lava and walls.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal G is visible two cells ahead and two cells to the right of my location. The cells immediately in front of me contain lava (L), so moving forward is not safe. I should turn right to face the open floor cells indicated, avoiding lava and moving towards the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I see the goal G one cell forward and to the left of my current position. However, there is lava L right next to the goal and a wall # blocking the direct path. The cell straight ahead of me is a wall, so I cannot move forward. I should turn left to face towards the gap on my left side that could lead me closer to the goal without stepping into lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing forward with my position at the bottom center. Ahead of me are lava cells ('L'), so moving forward would be deadly. To reach the goal 'G' that is to my right and slightly ahead, I will turn right to face that direction safely.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The goal G is visible two cells ahead and one cell to the left. In front of me is a wall, so moving forward is not possible. I need to turn left to face the direction of the goal as it is reachable that way.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal G is visible two cells ahead and two cells to the right of my location. The cells directly in front and to the right are lava, which I must avoid. The only safe move is to turn left and explore that direction to find a path toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I see a wall directly in front of me and to the sides as well, so moving forward is not possible. I should turn to find a possible path. Turning left might open a path to the left side where I could move forward.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
Ahead and around me are walls and lava cells, so moving forward is not safe. The walls block moving forward or to the sides from my current direction, so I will turn left to try to find a clear path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal G is visible two cells ahead and one cell to the left of my location. The cell directly in front of me is a floor cell (safe to move), but there is lava nearby to the left. To reach the goal safely, I should turn left first to face towards the goal and then move forward in the following steps.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
In front of me are two lava cells, which are deadly, so moving forward is not safe. To reach the goal G, which is visible to the right several cells ahead, I should turn right to face that direction and eventually move toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The goal G is visible two cells ahead and one cell to the left. There is lava L immediately to the left and forward-left diagonal, so I cannot move there safely. The cell straight ahead is a wall, so I cannot move forward. The cell to the immediate right is floor, so I will turn right to explore a safe path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>
